topic: Stuck in a listening loop / same songs on repeat
unmet_need: Gentle nudges that expand the rotation without losing comfort
research_question: Q4 repeat-listening of the same content
example_quotes: The same 50 songs on repeat problem is so real. I love them but I'm in a loop and the app does nothing to gently expand it.
A comfort-to-curiosity dial is genius. The biggest gap in every recommender is control over how far the recs stretch.
Stuck listening to the same 50 songs on repeat. The app never nudges me out of my comfort playlist, so I'm in a rut.
The killer feature would be adoption tracking. Most discovery never converts into a lasting habit a first play is not a win.
Stuck-in-a-loop is so real. People fall back on the same songs on repeat; the product that breaks that rut wins.
trying to get into jazz but i keep bouncing off it. i need a guided on-ramp not a 5 hour playlist someone help
stuck in a rut listening to the same songs on repeat and the algorithm refuses to break me out of the loop
Trying to get into classical as a total beginner and Spotify is no help. I need a beginner friendly path, not a firehose of 500 composers.

topic: New music never sticks (discovery without adoption)
unmet_need: Reinforcement that converts a first play into a lasting habit
research_question: Q4 / core: adoption, not one-off plays
example_quotes: I'd love a guided on-ramp to get into classical. Stage it for me week by week instead of one giant intimidating playlist.
I have ADHD and the firehose of new releases is completely overwhelming. Discovery is way too high-effort to keep up with.
As an aspirational explorer I save tons of new music and forget all of it. Retention and rotation are the real unmet need.
I keep trying to get into jazz but bounce off it every time. I need a guided on-ramp that eases me in week by week.
I open Discover Weekly, save a couple songs, and forget them by next week. High intent, nothing sticks. Story of my Spotify life.
New music never sticks for me. I play a recommended song once and forget it it never makes it into my rotation.
I save songs off Release Radar every week and forget them by the next. Nothing ever sticks into my rotation discovery without adoption.
I used to crate dig for hours. After kids I just loop the same comfort songs. I miss discovering but the effort barrier is too high now.

topic: No guided on-ramp into a new genre
unmet_need: Staged, goal-based exploration that coaches you into a genre
example_quotes: I love spotify! All of my favorite music can be played on spotify. I definitely recommend installing spotify!!!!!
I love Spotify but it randomly put songs I don't like in my Playlist and I had to download Spotify but I'm break so yeah 3 stars
very bad for free users, limited amount of skips and we cannot even change the songs if we want to. not recommended for free users.
Guided genre missions get into jazz over 3 weeks with a staged on-ramp would be a game changer for beginners.
Spotify has a very vast collection and ability to source the artists or songs that I love and enjoy. The lyrics, videos, and anything im looking for.
Genre exploration is intimidating. I'd pay for something that holds my hand into a new genre gradually and celebrates when I actually stick with an artist.
l like the playlist.. music n songs we enjoy to listen anywhere n everywhere
How do you actually get into a new genre? I want to get into jazz but I have no idea where to start and Spotify just throws a 200 song playlist at me with no guidance.

unmet_need: A comfort-to-curiosity control over how far recommendations stretch
example_quotes: Please add a slider to control how adventurous the recommendations are. Some days I want comfort, some days a real stretch.
Power digger here. I want deep cuts and obscure artists below a certain popularity, but the algorithm plays it way too safe.
I just want a control slider comfort versus curiosity instead of a fixed algorithm deciding how adventurous I get.
Honestly I listen to the same 50 songs on repeat. I want new music but I have zero energy to go looking for it after work and kids.
give me a dial for how adventurous my recs are. some days i want comfort, some days i want a real stretch
Busy mom, two kids, I want new music that fits my existing taste delivered to me with zero effort. I will not go digging. Bring it to me.
as a busy parent i have zero time to crate dig. i just want good new music to come to me with no effort
Discovery is overwhelming. There's too much music and no good way to ease into something new. I just give up and put on my liked songs again.

unmet_need: Genuinely novel recommendations that expand taste, not reinforce it
example_quotes: The algorithm is an echo chamber. It reinforces what I already play instead of expanding my taste into new artists.
Same here. The algorithm keeps recommending the same five artists over and over. It feels like an echo chamber, not discovery.
spotify discover weekly is just my own playlist read back to me at this point. same artists every single week, total echo chamber
This is exactly my problem Discover Weekly recycles the familiar. It's an echo chamber of the same five artists I already follow.
Love this. Spotify's discovery is an echo chamber it reinforces the taste you already have instead of expanding it into new artists.
Discover Weekly keeps recommending the same five artists every single week. It's an echo chamber and it never feels like real discovery.
My recommendations are an echo chamber. It just reinforces what I already listen to and never expands my horizons. I want growth not comfort food.

topic: Discovery surfaces don't lead anywhere new
unmet_need: Discovery that introduces artists you'd never have found yourself
example_quotes: discover weekly used to find me artists i'd never have found myself. now it's all safe picks i already know. discovery is broken
My discover weekly is stale. Every week it's the same vibe and the same handful of artists. I'm bored of it.
Discover Weekly used to be magic, now it just feeds me songs I already have saved. I never actually discover anything new from it anymore.
When Discover Weekly actually nails it, it's pure magic and honestly the reason I pay for premium.
Discover Weekly used to be magic, now it's the same five artists I already follow. The recommendations feel like a reminder feed of my own taste.
discover weekly archive. Automatically create a playlist that has all current, past (if possible), and any future discover weekly songs archived into one big playlist

I want to found back Last week "discover weekly". Not sure abut desktop but on Mobile... - Tap your profile - Tap recents - scroll down to 'discover weekly' : it has an 'expand' option showing the songs that were played in that week

Release Radar for Related Artists. It would be great to have a separate playlist similar to Release Radar that focuses on related artists of the artists I follow. This playlist could recommend newly released songs from artists that are musically similar, helping users discover new music while still matching their tastes

unmet_need: A clear bridge explaining why a track fits your existing taste
example_quotes: As a busy parent with two kids I have no time or energy to crate dig. I just want low-effort discovery that comes to me.
Honestly when a recommendation explains why a track fits my taste, I trust it so much more. The 'why' is everything.
The algorithm is too cautious. I'd love a slider for how adventurous the recommendations are, from safe to totally out there.
Discovery is a one shot thing. There's no sense of progress, no goal, no feeling that I'm actually growing my taste over time.
My problem is retention not discovery. I find cool new artists constantly, I just never build a lasting habit around any of them.
Good app but discovery recommends stuff so safe and cautious it's boring. I never find anything genuinely new.
Grounded 'why this' bridges build trust. Recommendations without a reason feel random and I never act on them.
Busy parents need zero-effort discovery that respects their time and comes to them instead of making them dig.
